name: Archie Bleyer
description: American song arranger, bandleader, and record company executive (1909–1989)
abstract: Archibald Martin Bleyer (June 12, 1909 – March 20, 1989) was an American song arranger, bandleader, and record company executive.

concerts.label: 1953 National String Festival and Convention Gala Youth Concert

works.t: Ein feste Burg ist unser Gott, BWV 720

concerts.label: Jazz and Variety Concert to Benefit The Lighthouse

about_quotes.speaker: Herman Autrey
about_quotes.evidence: Archie used to come in the club. He -- I can name you so many sitting there writing on the cuffs of their shirt. Him, me, and some other guy makes something

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: So he asked me if I would start a label. We called it Candid. He offered me an office... I had total freedom for about a year and a half or two.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: Archie Bleyer was writing a lot of things that had syncopation... that's the first time we seen -- and it was a school for me.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Archie Bleyer was instrumental in this of the chords and deals... I remember one of his stocks called Business in F.
